extra.message: User input from the 'name' parameter is concatenated directly into SQL string using string formatting (% operator). This allows SQL injection. The vulnerable code is: INSERT INTO students (name) VALUES ('%(name)s') % {'name': name}

Jinja2 autoescape is disabled globally with autoescape=False. This disables automatic HTML escaping for all templates, allowing any unescaped user input to be rendered as HTML/JavaScript, leading to Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The 'course.description' variable is rendered without escaping. Since autoescape is disabled in the application configuration, user-controlled course descriptions can contain malicious JavaScript that will be executed in the browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The 'course.description' variable is rendered without escaping. Since autoescape is disabled in the application configuration, user-controlled course descriptions can contain malicious JavaScript that will be executed in the browser.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: The 'review.review_text' variable is rendered without escaping. User-controlled review text can contain malicious JavaScript that will be executed in the browser when the page is viewed.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: MD5 is used for password hashing, which is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for further use. MD5 can be cracked easily with modern hardware and rainbow tables. Use bcrypt, scrypt, or Argon2 instead.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Database credentials (username and password) are hardcoded in the configuration file. This sensitive information should be stored in environment variables or a secure secret management system, not in version control.
